revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[de:Goshikizuka Kofun]][[en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun]][[ja:五色塚古墳]][[ko:고시키즈카 고분]][[zh:五色塚古墳]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Kobe, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Goshikizuka Kofun | native_name =五色塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Goshikizuka-kofun 3.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Goshikizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Goshikizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|46.6|N|135|2|45.08|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi-ku, Kobe|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =late 4th - mid-5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|46.6|N|135|2|45.08|E}}}}}} The {{nihongo|'''Goshikizuka Kofun'''|五色塚古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Goshikiyama neighborhood of {{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|en|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078806|lt=Tarumi-ku, Kobe|1=Tarumi-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus is the largest in Hyōgo Prefecture, and is estimated to have been built around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). It is known as the first ''kofun'' to be restored to what archaeologists believe to be its original appearance. It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Sentsubo Kofun'''|千壺古墳||}} . Together with the smaller neighboring {{nihongo|'''Kotsubo Kofun'''|小壺古墳||}} it was designated a National Historic Site in 1921, with the area under protection extended in 1974, 1979 and 2006.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210700|title=色塚（千壺）古墳 小壺古墳|trans-title= Goshikizuka Kofun (Sentsubo Kofun) Kotsubo Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Overview== The Goshikizuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located at the southern end of the Tarumi hill in the western part of Kobe city, overlooking the Akashi Strait on the [[Seto Inland Sea]] and {{ill|Awaji Island|en|Awaji Island|qid=Q504373|lt=Awaji Island|1=Awaji Island}}, and is orientated to the south. The origins the name "Goshikizuka" is unknown, but dates to at least the early [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], when the tumulus was believed to have been the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Chuai|Emperor Chuai]]. The name "sentsubo" is from the vast number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' on the site. The tumulus area was protected for centuries as it was in a pine forest; however, the trees were harvested in World War II and the area was cleared for fields and urban development after the end of the war. The tracks of the {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=San'yō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} railway passing's front of anterior portion of the tumulus, and construction of roads has destroyed a portion of the surrounding moats.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} Structurally, the tumulus is built in three tiers. The length of the tumulus is 194 meters. Cylindrical ''haniwa'' rows are laid on each tier of the mound, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' on each step slope. The number of ''haniwa'' is estimated to be over 2200 and the number of ''fukiishi'' is estimated to be approximately 2.23 million (2784 tons), with the upper and middlestones coming from the northeastern coast of Awaji Island. The tumulus is surrounded by double deep moats with shallow external ditches, and inside the moat are three square island-shaped mounds on the left and right of the main tumulus constriction and northeast of the posterior circular portion. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is not clear, but it is believed to be a pit-type stone chamber as fragments of crystalline {{ill|Schist|en|Schist|qid=Q15315|lt=Schist|1=Schist}} originating in {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}} have been found. It is unclear for whom this tomb was constructed, but it is believed to have been for a ruler who controlled both sides of Akashi Strait. In construction method and scale, it is comparable to the royal tombs of {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|qid=Q633881|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato period}}. The dimensions of the tumulus are almost identical to the Sakiryoyama Kofun in Nara. As the surrounding area does not appear to have had the economic base for such a construction, it is also theorized that this indicates a strong connection with the Yamato Kingdom. The ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mentions a large tumulus being constructed in this area during the reign of {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}, but states that it was a "fake tomb" without an actual burial. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 1965 to 1974, 1977, and 1982 to 1993. In 2012, the excavated items (the cylindrical ''haniwa'' group) were collectively designated as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. Most of the excavated items are stored at the Kobe City Archaeological Center.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011540|title=兵庫県五色塚古墳出土品|trans-title= Hyōgo Prefecture Goshikizuka Kofun Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020}} ==Kotsubo Kofun== The Kotsubo Kofun is large circular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style mound, with a diameter of 70 meters, and height of 8.5 meters, making it the second largest ''enpun'' in the prefecture after the [[Chasuriyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Asago, Hyōgo|en|Asago, Hyōgo|qid=Q720402|lt=Asago|1=Asago, Hyōgo}}. It was constructed in two tiers and was surrounded by a moat. It had an estimated 320 ''haniwa'' in two rows, including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and figurative ''haniwa'', but unlike the Goshikizuka Kofun, no ''fukiishi'' were found. From the ''haniwa'', the tumulus is estimated to be contemporary with the Goshikizuka Kofun, or from around the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century, but it is not clear which was constructed first. According to literature, the surrounding area once had a number of other large ''enpun'' Kofuns, but these have since been destroyed. The site is open to the public as an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and is located about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Kasumigaoka Station (Hyōgo)|en|Kasumigaoka Station (Hyōgo)|qid=Q6374899|lt=Kasumigaoka Station|1=Kasumigaoka Station (Hyōgo)}} on the {{ill|Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line|en|Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line|qid=Q871058|lt=Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line|1=Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line}} or 15-minutes from [[:en:Tarumi Station (Hyogo)|Tarumi Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=San'yō Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ !name !location !type !Size |- |'''Goshikizuka Kofun'''||{{coord|34|37|46.6|N|135|2|45.08|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||length 194m
posterior circular portion: 125.5 m dia
anterior width:82.4m |- |'''Kotsubo Kofun'''||{{coord|34|37|49|N|135|2|41.49|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|}}||Circular||dia 70.0m
height 8.5m |} ==Gallery== Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg| Goshikizuka Kofun Goshiki-zuka Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|Goshikizuka Kofun, ''haniwa'' and ''fukiishi'' Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|Awaji Island and Akashi Strait from Goshikuzuka Kofun 五色塚古墳出土 埴輪.JPG|''Haniwa'' from Goshikizuka Kofun Kotsubo Kofun, zenkei.jpg|Kotsubo Kofun ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|qid=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Goshikizuka kofun}} *[https://goshikiduka.org Official home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/a21651/kanko/bunka/bunkazai/estate/bunkazai/syokai/goshiki.html Kobe City home page] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} }} {{draft categories| [[en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q1538541}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
